summary: First-pass full-stack testing for SwipeNight. Backend: all 30 pytest tests pass (100%) — auth (login, register, /me, dup-email), onboarding (preferences + calibration 20), recommendations (home hero + rails, 5 context variants, /reasons), browse (filter+search+sort), content detail (match_score, reasons, reviews, community_rating, user_state), events (like persists state), watchlist add/get/remove, reviews (public visible to others, private hidden), rooms full flow (create→start→candidates→superlike vote→winner→relaunch round 2), join MOVIE1, privacy defaults/update, dev daily job. Frontend (mobile viewport 390x844): seeded alex_noir → home feed renders hero+rails with match scores; content detail → rate & review sheet works; navigation between tabs OK; Rooms tab shows Friday Movie Night with MOVIE1 code; Join room screen joins demo room successfully showing lobby with members + code + threshold/quorum + Start swiping button; new-user register → onboarding (preferences with platform/format/genre chips) → calibration deck (deck-like + skip) → home tabs; browse search by 'the' returns filtered grid. Solo room creation screen renders correctly. No critical bugs, no red screen errors. Minor non-blocking issues: Fraunces font files return HTML instead of OTF (decode warnings), React 19 element.ref deprecation warning, passlib trapped bcrypt __about__ AttributeError in backend log.

minor.issue: passlib emits 'AttributeError: module bcrypt has no attribute __about__' (trapped) due to passlib<=1.7.4 vs bcrypt>=4 version mismatch. Hashing still works. Consider pinning bcrypt<4 or migrating to passlib >=1.7.5/argon2 to clean logs.

issues: Custom Fraunces font files (Fraunces-Regular/Medium/SemiBold.ttf) fail to load — endpoint returns HTML, causing 'OTS parsing error: invalid sfntVersion' warnings. App falls back to system fonts so visuals are acceptable, but the brand font does not render. Likely an Expo Router web static-asset path issue (the URL contains ?unstable_path=./assets/fonts/...).

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning 'Accessing element.ref was removed in React 19. ref is now a regular prop'. Coming from a library (likely react-native-reanimated or expo-router). Non-blocking but should be tracked for SDK upgrade.

action_items: (Optional) Fix Fraunces font asset serving on web so the cinematic typography renders; verify font URL works without ?unstable_path= prefix or pre-bundle the OTF.
(Optional) Resolve passlib/bcrypt log noise by pinning bcrypt<4 in requirements.txt.
No business-logic fixes required — all core flows green.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is large (~1100 lines). Consider splitting into routers (auth, contents, rooms, reviews, events) for maintainability.
DELETE /api/watchlist/{id} returns 200 even when no doc exists — acceptable, but could 404 for clarity.
Room start does not check membership beyond owner; non-owner gets 403 (good). Rooms have no leave endpoint — minor gap.
WebSocket /api/ws/rooms/{id} has no auth handshake; in production should validate JWT.
Reviews list endpoint runs an N+1 user lookup (one db.users.find_one per review). Fine at MVP scale (<=50 reviews) but consider $lookup or in-memory cache later.
seed_data_creation: Tests register TEST_<uuid>@swipenight.app accounts and a TEST_solo_room; reviews prefixed with 'TEST'. No cleanup performed (acceptable for non-prod test DB).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (frontend/.env), not EXPO_BACKEND_URL. Seeded users login with password123 and are pre-onboarded (skip prefs/calibration). New users must select >=1 platform+format+genre then complete or skip calibration before home. Demo room code: MOVIE1 (alex_noir owner, Sam & Rae members). For solo winner determinism create room with quorum=1, threshold_percent=50. WebSocket path is /api/ws/rooms/{id}. The full room flow (create→start→candidates→vote→result with winner→relaunch) is covered in test_full_room_flow.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects discovered this iteration.
